symptoms.symptom_name: Symmetric MCP/PIP joint pain and swelling

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Symmetric small-joint involvement is characteristic of RA.
Septic arthritis is typically monoarticular, not symmetric.

symptoms.symptom_name: No single hot red joint; afebrile

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of a single hot red joint and fever argues strongly against septic arthritis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Overuse trigger in known seronegative RA; passive motion not agonising

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A clear overuse trigger on known RA supports a mechanical flare.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Routine GP review ± rheumatology referral and inflammatory markers

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Only if a single hot joint/fever suggests septic arthritis — not indicated for a symmetric afebrile flare.
